audit_items.proof_trace: In `ldap_ldif_record_done` (L78-79), `lr->lr_freeval[i]` is dereferenced inside a loop without a NULL guard on `lr->lr_freeval`. The invariant fails when `ldap_parse_ldif_record_x` sets `lr->lr_lines` via `ldif_countlines` (L135) then returns LDAP_NO_MEMORY via early `return` (L138) before ever setting `lr_freeval`, leaving `lr_lines > 0` and `lr_freeval == NULL`. Callers such as `ldapmodify.c:453` call `ldap_ldif_record_done` unconditionally after any return code, triggering the NPD.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker supplies a multi-line LDIF record to `ldapmodify` while causing (or waiting for) memory pressure so that `ber_memcalloc_x` fails; `ldapmodify.c` unconditionally calls `ldap_ldif_record_done`, which crashes at the NULL `lr_freeval` dereference, producing a denial-of-service.
